topics: 🐂 Slaughtering a black ox in the forest as a חזקה of theft
🔪 Differences between טובח and מוכר — Shabbat liability
🧩 Selling 'except for' a part: when does ד״ה or ארבעה וחמישה apply
🚪 Drawing the animal out vs. lifting it — kinyan and liability for ד׳ וה׳
🐑 Bans on raising small livestock, chickens, pigs, and dogs in Eretz Yisrael

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Stolen Sales, Sneaky Slaughters, and the Settlement
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf closes out perek 7 of Bava Kamma. It refines the Mishnah's rules on ארבעה וחמישה: what counts as a complete sale, what counts as a complete slaughter, and exactly when the thief's act becomes binding. From there the Yerushalmi turns to the very different — but related — world of where animals may be raised: the bans on small livestock, chickens, pigs, and unchained dogs in Eretz Yisrael, and where one may set pigeon traps.
overview.hebrewSource: אָמַר רִבִּי לָעְזָר. רָאוּ אוֹתוֹ שׁוֹחֵט שׁוֹר שָׁחוֹר בַּיַּעַר חֲזָקָה גָּנוּב הוּא

slides.title: 🌲 The Black Ox in the Forest
slides.subtitle: R' Lazar's chazakah of theft and the Yerushalmi's challenge.

lines.speaker: RL
lines.text: רָאוּ אוֹתוֹ שׁוֹחֵט שׁוֹר שָׁחוֹר בַּיַּעַר חֲזָקָה גָּנוּב הוּא — slaughtering a black ox in the forest is presumptive evidence of theft.

lines.speaker: Q
lines.text: But maybe the ox was already lying down? A passerby could simply slaughter an animal he found resting.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: תִּיפְתָּר בְּשֶׁרָאוּהוּ מוֹשֵׁךְ — the ruling speaks of a case where they saw him dragging it. Then the chazakah of theft holds.

callout.label: Reading the move:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi tightens R' Lazar's principle by adding a behavioral fingerprint: a thief drags the animal out of sight before slaughtering. The pasture context plus the dragging is what triggers the chazakah.

slides.title: 🔪 Slaughterer vs. Seller
slides.subtitle: Each act has a feature the other lacks.

boxes.title: 🔪 הטובח
items: Slaughtering is a melachah on Shabbat

boxes.title: 💰 המוכר
items: Sale is not in itself a melachah
But still completes the kinyan for ד׳ וה׳

boxes.title: 🌱 R' Yannai's edge case
items: ‘תְּלוֹשׁ מִן הַקַּרְקַע הַזֶּה וּקְנֵה אוֹתוֹ’
R' Yose b. R' Bun: it's the tearing, not the sale, that creates the criminal liability

callout.label: Why the comparison:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi is mapping the unique halakhic profile of each act so neither can simply be derived from the other — the same logical move it made earlier between שור and בור.

slides.title: 📊 Selling 'Except For…' — Three Tannaitic Positions
slides.subtitle: When does an incomplete sale still trigger ארבעה וחמישה?
rows: חוּץ מִדָּבָר שֶׁהַנְשָׁמָה תְלוּיָה בּוֹ — ד׳ וה׳

callout.label: R' Ze'ira's question:
callout.text: מָכַר חֶצְיָיהּ לָזֶה וְחֶצְיָיהּ לָזֶה מָהוּ? — what about selling halves to two different people? The Yerushalmi answers from the beraita: any retained share normally still leaves the seller liable for ד׳ וה׳, unless Rebbi's vital-organ test fails.

slides.title: 🚪 When Does the Thief Become Liable for ד׳ וה׳?
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah of הָיָה מוֹשְׁכוֹ וְיוֹצֵא.

nodes.content: Thief is moving the stolen animal — and it dies en route
R' Mana's question: does פָּטוּר here mean exempt from ד׳ וה׳ — or exempt even from the basic theft itself?
The Mishnah reaches the same result whether the thief acts personally or hands it to a שומר / לוקח / שואל — the kinyan-act is what counts.

callout.label: Key insight:
callout.text: ד׳ וה׳ is gated by a completed kinyan of theft. הגבהה or removal seal it; mere drawing on the owner's premises does not.

slides.title: 🐑 The Final Mishnah: Where Animals May Be Raised
slides.subtitle: A sharp pivot from theft into community-protective halakhot.

steps.content: 1️⃣ בהמה דקה: forbidden in Eretz Yisrael — but permitted in Syria and in מדברות שבארץ ישראל (R' Abba: e.g. Mahir, 16×16 mil).
2️⃣ תרנגולין in Yerushalayim: forbidden because of the קדשים they would defile by digging up impure things.
3️⃣ Cohanim anywhere in EY: forbidden, because of the טהרות they handle.
4️⃣ חזירים: a Jew may not raise pigs anywhere — full stop.
5️⃣ כלב: only permitted if קָשׁוּר בַּשַׁלְשֶׁלֶת; נשבים ליונים only 30 ris from any settlement.

callout.label: R' Yehoshua b. Levi's lens:
callout.text: He ties the chicken ban to יְרוּשָׁלַ͏ִם הַבְּנוּיָה — עִיר שֶׁמְחַבֶּרֶת יִשְׂרָאֵל זֶה לָזֶה. Yerushalayim is a city built around shared sanctity, so its rules protect that fabric.

summary.principle: Perek 7 closes by tightening when ד׳ וה׳ applies — completed sale, completed slaughter, completed kinyan — and then turns to the social geography of which animals may be raised where in Eretz Yisrael.

flowSteps.content:
Open question:
When does a thief's act become a complete טביחה ומכירה?

columns.content:
🌲 Public chazakah
Dragging an ox in the forest = stolen

flowSteps.content: Pivot: Mishnah of livestock bans, chickens in Yerushalayim, pigs anywhere, dogs only on a chain.

🎓 Key Principle:
Liability follows completed acts; community halakhot guard the agricultural and sacral fabric of Eretz Yisrael.

quiz.q: What does R' Lazar say about someone seen slaughtering a black ox in the forest?
o: He is exempt because the forest is הפקר
חֲזָקָה גָּנוּב הוּא — there is a presumption it was stolen
He must bring two witnesses
He pays only כפל
quiz.e: R' Lazar establishes a chazakah of theft, and the Yerushalmi clarifies that this applies specifically when he was seen dragging it — בְּשֶׁרָאוּהוּ מוֹשֵׁךְ.

quiz.q: What unique liability does a slaughterer have that a seller does not?
o: He pays double
He is criminally liable on Shabbat (slaughter is a melachah)
He cannot be a שליח
He must reimburse the מוכר
quiz.e: הַטּוֹבֵחַ בַּשַׁבָּת חַיָיב וְהַמּוֹכֵר בַּשַׁבָּת פָּטוּר. R' Yose b. R' Bun explains the apparent counter-case: it's the tearing from the ground, not the sale itself, that creates Shabbat liability.

quiz.q: According to Rebbi, when does selling 'except for X' still trigger ד׳ וה׳?
o: Only if X is the horn
Only if X is the wool
When X is a vital organ — דָּבָר שֶׁהַנְשָׁמָה תְלוּיָה בּוֹ
Only when X is sold to a third party
quiz.e: Rebbi's test: if the retained part is a vital organ, the buyer's purchase is meaningful and ד׳ וה׳ applies; if not (e.g. wool, horn), the seller is exempt.

quiz.q: If a thief was drawing the animal out but it died while still on the owner's property, what is the law?
o: Liable for ד׳ וה׳
פָּטוּר — the kinyan of theft is not yet complete
Liable for double only
Liable only if there were witnesses
quiz.e: The Mishnah teaches that drawing alone, while still on the owner's premises, does not complete the theft. The thief is liable only after הגבהה or once it leaves the property — and R' Mana even asks whether the exemption extends to basic theft as well.

quiz.q: Which is NOT one of the Mishnah's restrictions on raising animals?
o: אֵין מְגַדְּלִין בְּהֵמָה דַקָּה בְּאֶרֶץ יִשְׂרָאֵל
אֵין מְגַדְּלִין תַּרְנְגוֹלִין בִּירוּשָׁלַיִם
לֹא יְגַדֵּל אָדָם אֶת הַכֶּלֶב אֶלָּא אִם כֵּן הָיָה קָשׁוּר בַּשַׁלְשֶׁלֶת
אֵין מְגַדְּלִין סוּסִים בְּסוּרְיָא
quiz.e: The Mishnah bans small livestock in EY (permitted in Syria and the מדברות), chickens in Yerushalayim and for kohanim anywhere, pigs everywhere for a Jew, and unchained dogs. It says nothing about horses in Syria.
